CONTENT_ADDRESS.description: Asset bytes are resolved by content address, usually an IPFS CID.

SDN_QUERY.description: Asset bytes are resolved from the provider through a signed SDN query protocol. This mode is used when the update is not published as a globally discoverable file.

OTHER.description: Asset bytes are resolved by another protocol named in TRANSPORT_PROTOCOL.

DPMCompletenessIndex.description: Completeness-capable signed index over a dataset update.

INDEX_NAME.description:
Stable index name, e.g. file_id, norad_cat_id, epoch, source_batch. Every
completeness-verifiable dataset update SHOULD include a file_id index so
subscribers can prove that all returned records belong to the announced
FILE_ID partition.

CANONICAL_ORDER.description:
Deterministic ordering expression for the index. Providers and
subscribers MUST use this ordering when building or verifying range
proofs. A query is completeness-verifiable only when its predicate can be
expressed against one or more declared indexes.

INDEX_ROOT.description:
SHA-256 or Merkle root of the ordered index, lowercase hex. This root is
signed by the DPM provider signature and is the verifier's commitment for
inclusion and range-completeness proofs. To verify a provider-mediated
response, the subscriber recomputes each returned leaf, walks the supplied
sibling hashes using MERKLE_PROFILE, confirms the root equals INDEX_ROOT,
and confirms any range-boundary proofs required by CANONICAL_ORDER.

MERKLE_PROFILE.description:
Hash profile for leaves and internal nodes, e.g.
SDN-MERKLE-SHA256-v1. Profiles define domain separators, leaf material,
pair ordering, duplicate handling, and odd-leaf promotion.

SUPPORTS_RANGE_COMPLETENESS.description:
Whether this index can prove that no matching records were omitted for a
supported range query. Inclusion proofs alone prove authenticity, not
completeness.

DPMAsset.description:
One immutable asset or provider-mediated query contract published for a
dataset update.

TRANSPORT_KIND.description:
Transport profile for this asset. CONTENT_ADDRESS assets use CID and
MULTIFORMAT_ADDRESS. SDN_QUERY assets use TRANSPORT_PROTOCOL plus the
signed DPM query and root fields; they are not required to be published as
discoverable IPFS files.

CONTENT_ADDRESS.description: Asset bytes are resolved by content address, usually an IPFS CID.

SDN_QUERY.description: Asset bytes are resolved from the provider through a signed SDN query protocol. This mode is used when the update is not published as a globally discoverable file.

OTHER.description: Asset bytes are resolved by another protocol named in TRANSPORT_PROTOCOL.

CID.description:
Optional IPFS CIDv1/multihash content identifier. This field is required
for CONTENT_ADDRESS assets and SHOULD be empty for SDN_QUERY assets whose
bytes are retrieved through a provider protocol.

MULTIFORMAT_ADDRESS.description:
Multiformat address. For CONTENT_ADDRESS this is usually /ipfs/{CID}. For
SDN_QUERY this MAY be a provider peer multiaddr, relay hint, or empty when
provider routing is resolved from the DPM provider identity.

FILE_NAME.description: File name or logical artifact name.

FILE_ID.description:
Canonical publication/update partition identity for this asset. FILE_ID is
not a display filename; it is the stable identifier used by PNMs,
manifests, entitlements, query requests, subscriber caches, and
completeness proofs. Example:
celestrak:gp:OMM.fbs:2026-05-06T03:00:00Z.

TRANSPORT_PROTOCOL.description:
Provider protocol name/version used to fetch this asset when
TRANSPORT_KIND is SDN_QUERY, e.g. /sdn/dataset-query/1.0.0. The protocol
response MUST be verifiable against DATA_ROOT, INDEXES, QUERY, and the
provider signature in this DPM.

BYTE_LENGTH.description: Byte length of the published object.

BYTE_SHA256.description: SHA-256 hash of the exact published bytes, lowercase hex.

DATA_ROOT.description:
Merkle root over canonical records in this asset, lowercase hex. For
provider-mediated query delivery, subscribers verify returned records and
proof paths against this root before importing data.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: SDS schema name for data artifacts, e.g. OMM.fbs, CAT.fbs, SPW.fbs.

SCHEMA_HASH.description: Hash of the SDS schema used to encode this object.

CONTENT_KEY_ID.description: Optional content-key identifier for encrypted artifacts.

DPMSourceBatch.description: Source batch metadata bound into the dataset publication.

SOURCE_SHA256.description: SHA-256 hash of raw source bytes, lowercase hex.

HTTP_LAST_MODIFIED.description: HTTP Last-Modified value observed during retrieval.

RECORD_COUNT.description: Number of normalized records emitted from this source.

WARNINGS.description: Warnings or policy notes produced during normalization.

DPMQueryBinding.description: Canonical query metadata that can replay the dataset selection.

CANONICAL_QUERY.description: Canonical query text or canonical JSON representation.

QUERY_SHA256.description: SHA-256 hash of CANONICAL_QUERY, lowercase hex.

RESULT_SHA256.description: SHA-256 hash of the ordered result bytes, lowercase hex.

QUERY_ENGINE.description: Query engine or profile name, e.g. FlatSQL.

QUERY_ENGINE_VERSION.description: Query engine version or profile version.

CANONICAL_ORDER.description:
Canonical ordering of result records before RESULT_SHA256 or DATA_ROOT is
computed. Providers MUST stream records in this order unless each chunk
includes enough proof material to restore and verify the canonical order.

QUERY_PROTOCOL.description:
Query protocol name/version for provider-mediated retrieval, e.g.
/sdn/dataset-query/1.0.0. A subscriber verifies the PNM and DPM, opens this
protocol to the provider, submits the signed query or a permitted subset,
and imports only responses that verify against the signed roots.

SCHEMA_NAMES.description: SDS schema names selected by the query.

PROVIDER_IDS.description: Provider peer IDs selected by the query.

SOURCE_NAMES.description: Source names selected by the query.

BATCH_IDS.description: Batch IDs selected by the query.

WINDOW_START.description: Inclusive query window start in ISO 8601 UTC.

WINDOW_END.description: Inclusive query window end in ISO 8601 UTC.

DPMEncryptionBinding.description: Encryption metadata for protected dataset artifacts.

ENCRYPTED.description: Whether any artifact in this manifest is encrypted.

POLICY_ID.description: Policy or listing identifier bound as AAD.

AAD_SHA256.description: SHA-256 hash of canonical encryption AAD, lowercase hex.

DPM.description:
Dataset Publication Manifest binding data/index CIDs, query replay,
source hashes, schema hashes, encryption metadata, provider-mediated query
protocols, completeness roots, and provider signature.

FILE_ID.description:
Canonical publication/update partition identity. FILE_ID is the key used
everywhere a subscriber, provider, PNM, entitlement, cache, audit log, or
query protocol refers to this exact update. It is not merely a human
filename and it is not the FlatBuffer file_identifier. For
completeness-verifiable streams, all returned records MUST belong to this
FILE_ID and prove inclusion under this DPM's signed roots, normally through
a declared file_id completeness index.

ASSETS.description: Published shard, index, and auxiliary artifacts.

SOURCES.description: Source batches used to build the dataset.

INDEXES.description:
Signed completeness-capable indexes. Inclusion proofs prove that returned
records are authentic members of DATA_ROOT. Range-completeness proofs also
prove that no matching records were omitted, but only for predicates
expressible against these declared indexes.

PROVIDER_SIGNATURE.description: Provider signature over the canonical manifest bytes or manifest digest.
